title: moriera spinosa subsp. cabulica
intro: Moriera spinosa subsp. cabulica, also known as the Afghan boxwood or Afghan box hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Moriera spinosa subsp. cabulica include treating various ailments such as fever, colds, and digestive issues, as well as supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve boiling the leaves and branches to make a tea or infusion, which is then consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
